assumptions.note: Token-metered models do not publish a fixed tokens-per-image figure, so the 'est. cost / 1024x1024 image' column applies these representative token counts uniformly to every token-priced model for a like-for-like comparison. Real cost scales with resolution, quality and prompt length.

gpt-image-2.quota: Per-subscription TPM/RPM, tiered; raise via an Azure quota-increase request
gpt-image-2.throughput: Image deployments start with a modest images-per-minute allowance that scales with assigned TPM/RPM quota

azure_measured.limit_type: RPM only (no separate token bucket on this image deployment)

flux-2-pro.quota: Global Standard shared quota pool per subscription (not per-region)
flux-2-pro.throughput: Per-subscription RPM/TPM against the shared Global Standard pool; confirm the model SKU default in the portal
regions: Global Standard (shared) — see Foundry region matrix
flux-2-pro.source: Azure AI Foundry Models pricing — Black Forest Labs

MAI-Image-2.quota: Foundry first-party quota; managed per subscription (see model card)
MAI-Image-2.throughput: Optimized for high-volume / always-on workloads; ~2x faster than the prior generation per Microsoft
regions: Microsoft Foundry first-party (Global Standard) — see Foundry region matrix
MAI-Image-2.source: Microsoft AI blog — 3 new MAI models available in Foundry

MAI-Image-2.5.deployment: Microsoft Foundry (first-party MAI); MAI-Image-2.5 Flash available for faster/cheaper runs
MAI-Image-2.5.quota: Foundry first-party quota; managed per subscription (see model card)
MAI-Image-2.5.throughput: Flash variant targets fast, scalable production workloads; best price-to-performance ELO per Microsoft
regions: Microsoft Foundry first-party (Global Standard) — see Foundry region matrix
MAI-Image-2.5.source: Microsoft Foundry — new MAI models (Build 2026), MAI-Image-2.5 Foundry model card

capacity_note: azure_measured values are the request-per-minute (RPM) limits actually configured on the test deployments (Global Standard, Sweden Central) at the time the latencies were recorded, read from Azure. They are the per-deployment defaults for this subscription and can be raised via a quota request; they are not vendor-wide maximums. These image deployments are RPM-limited and do not expose a separate TPM bucket.
